title: Benzoin touyunense
taxonomy: Benzoin touyunense belongs to the family Styracaceae. This species is a type of tree native to the mountainous regions of China. It is closely related to other species within the genus Styrax. The plant's classification places it within the order Ericales. Further classification at the subfamily level would be within the Styracoideae subfamily.
uses: Benzoin touyunense is a lesser-known species of resinous tree, but research suggests its potential medicinal uses. The resin of this tree may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could contribute to wound healing and skin health. Further studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions. Benzoin touyunense may also have antioxidant properties, which could help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, the full range of its medicinal uses and potential benefits require more extensive research.

constituents: Benzoin touyunense contains active constituents that include resins, specifically benzoin resin, which is a complex mixture of compounds including benzyl benzoate, benzyl cinnamate, and benzyl salicylate. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal and aromatic properties. The resin is also known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
